title: Bump Tempo to v2.10.7 (currently pinned at v2.10.3)
summary: The newest rockcraft.yaml (2.10.3/rockcraft.yaml) pins Tempo to v2.10.3, but upstream stable latest is v2.10.7. Patch-level bump picking up upstream fixes (including some CVE fixes flagged in the repo's auto-issues).
why_good_first_issue: Mechanical patch bump following the repo's per-version-subdirectory convention; only version + source-tag change; clear upstream comparison.
repro_steps: gh api repos/canonical/tempo-rock/contents/2.10.3/rockcraft.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d; gh api repos/grafana/tempo/releases/latest --jq .tag_name
repro_result: rockcraft.yaml has version: "2.10.3" and source-tag: "v2.10.3"; upstream releases/latest = v2.10.7 (prerelease=false, published 2026-06-12). Tags v2.10.4/v2.10.5/v2.10.6/v2.10.7 all exist. Rock lags upstream by four patch releases.
fix_hint: Create a 2.10.7/ directory mirroring 2.10.3/ (copy rockcraft.yaml + any spread tests), set version: "2.10.7" and source-tag: "v2.10.7". Keep go/1.25 build-snap unless upstream bumped Go requirement.
notes: Per-version subdirectory layout. Open issues (#61-#63) are auto-generated CVE reports; the bump partially addresses the prometheus/prometheus CVEs referenced there but is primarily a routine version-bump.
